rationale_for_baseline_tier: Uncomplicated viral laryngitis with a negative airway screen (no stridor, drooling, dysphagia for saliva, tripod posture or toxicity) is self-limiting and managed with voice rest and hydration — self-care (T0). A hard airway safety-net to T5 is mandatory because epiglottitis/tracheitis are the dangerous mimics.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Noisy/whistling breathing (stridor), difficulty breathing, drooling, inability to swallow saliva, muffled 'hot-potato' voice, or sitting forward to breathe
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicates supraglottic airway obstruction (epiglottitis) — a minutes-matter airway emergency.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your breathing becomes noisy or difficult, you start drooling or can't swallow your saliva, or you have to sit forward to breathe — call 000 immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: High fever with systemic toxicity or rapidly worsening severe throat pain
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests bacterial tracheitis/epiglottitis needing urgent assessment.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives with family who can observe and assist
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: A household member can monitor for airway deterioration overnight and call 000 if needed.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: You can look after this at home: rest your voice (don't whisper), drink plenty of fluids, use a humidifier and take paracetamol for soreness. It should ease over the next week or two.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Self-care with GP review at 7–10 days if hoarseness persists; 000/ED for any airway features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 is available 24/7 for breathing difficulty; otherwise self-care overnight.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Acute viral laryngitis, airway screen negative; voice rest and hydration advised; laryngoscopy if hoarseness >2–3 weeks.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring without giving an airway safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Early epiglottitis progresses overnight; patient does not recognise stridor as an emergency and obstructs before reaching care.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Not screening for stridor/drooling/dysphagia before reassurance.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Referring an uncomplicated viral laryngitis with a negative airway screen to ED — unnecessary.
